message: test(conformance): add row multiindex live oracle coverage (br-frankenpandas-1zzp.5)

files.patch:
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.2","title":"[EPIC slice 2/6] GroupBy multi-key emit real row MultiIndex (retire 'a|1' flattening)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6 (DataFrame row-axis type refactor).

groupby([k1, k2]).sum() / .mean() / .agg_* currently emit string-flattened keys (s:a|i:1 per FP-P2C-005 / FP-P2C-011) into a flat Index<IndexLabel>. br-frankenpandas-r4au tried to land tuple emission for grouped rolling/resample and was reverted in br-ch4o back to the flat workaround because DataFrame can't carry a row MultiIndex (slice 1). Once slice 1 ships, this slice:

1. Changes DataFrameGroupBy result construction to emit a row MultiIndex whose levels[0..n] are the n group-by key columns in order (rather than a single Utf8 column with 'k1_val|k2_val' strings).
2. Strips the flatten-sep codepath in fp-frame/src/lib.rs groupby aggregation builders; existing separator utilities kept for stringify fallbacks (.to_flat_index) but no longer in the hot path.
3. Touches every groupby aggfunc return path: sum, mean, min, max, count, std, var, median, first, last, prod, any, all, sem, skew, kurtosis, ohlc, idxmin, idxmax, nunique, cumcount, ngroup, get_group, ffill, bfill, agg / agg_list / agg_dict_list / agg_named / agg_multi.
4. Retires fp-conformance packet fixtures for FP-P2C-005 / FP-P2C-011 that pin the flat-index legacy output; new live-oracle fixtures assert pandas parity against real MultiIndex output.
5. Updates the MultiIndex README section (README.md:396) to drop the 'currently flattens ... a|1' qualifier once this lands.

Acceptance: groupby([k1, k2]).sum() output on an oracle-seeded frame equals pandas' output under DataFrame::equals / semantic_eq AND carries a real row MultiIndex (not a string-flattened Index). Both existing and new conformance suites green under rch.","notes":"cc-pandas landed slice 2 via commit 7a503ae. DataFrameGroupBy::format_output (fp-frame/src/lib.rs:25945) now calls the new group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper and threads Option<MultiIndex> into DataFrame::new_with_axes instead of the direct struct literal with row_multiindex: None. The helper returns None when self.by.len() <= 1 (flat Index is already correct), otherwise builds one level-array per group-by column across the aggregated groups, preserving Int64 / Utf8 / Timedelta64 / Bool variants and falling back to Debug format for other scalars; names the levels from by. Covers all 9 format_output callers (sum / mean / min / max / count / std / var / median / agg*). as_index=false path intentionally untouched — pandas semantics are 'group keys become regular columns, row index is a default int range' there. Flat Index storage (group_key_label's comma-joined labels) preserved as fallback so external consumers that read index() get the same values as before. Two new tests under fp-frame lib: groupby_sum_multikey_attaches_row_multiindex + groupby_agg_named_multikey_attaches_row_multiindex — both assert row_multiindex() is Some, levels and names match the group-by columns, row_index() returns MultiIndexOrIndex::Multi, and aggregated values are correct. Tests: cargo test -p fp-frame --lib → 1405/0; cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib → 388/0; packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases + differential_all_packets_green both pass. Slice 3 (tuple .loc / .xs / .get_loc) is unblocked — dispatch can check row_multiindex() on groupby output frames. Slice 4 (reshape round-trips) can compose — df.groupby([a, b]).sum().reset_index() becomes natural once reset_index learns to read row_multiindex().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:45.810040165Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:28:16.408458101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:28:16.406037816Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-1zzp-2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","groupby","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.2","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:45.810040165Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.3","title":"[EPIC slice 3/6] Tuple-key .loc + .xs + .get_loc on row MultiIndex","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Pandas indexing API surface on MultiIndex row axes (matches pd.DataFrame.loc / xs / get_loc):

1. df.loc[(a, 1)] — exact tuple-key lookup on a 2-level row MultiIndex; returns a row Series (or DataFrame if multiple hits).
2. df.loc[(a, slice(None))] / df.loc[idx[('a', :)]] — level slicing; pandas uses pd.IndexSlice / . Provide a Rust equivalent (e.g. IdxSlice builder) that produces a TupleKeyPattern the lookup path can honor.
3. df.xs(key, level=N) — returns a frame with the N-th level dropped, selecting only rows where level[N] == key.
4. df.get_loc((a, 1)) / df.get_loc(key, level=Some(0)) — returns the row position(s) for a tuple or partial tuple key.
5. df.index.get_loc_level(tuple) — pandas-style partial-tuple lookup returning (positions, remaining_index).
6. MultiIndex.slice_locs((start_tuple, end_tuple)) — pandas contract for sorted-MI range slicing.

Error semantics: match pandas KeyError for unknown tuples; match pandas TypeError for wrong-arity tuples. Live-oracle fixtures under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/fp_p2c_[new]_multi_loc_*.json per lookup variant.

Acceptance: all 6 surfaces above pass against pd.read_csv(tuple_fixture) → compare against live pandas oracle via the existing assert_live_oracle_* harness.","notes":"cc-pandas verified parallel agent's commit 5295c03 implements the slice 3 spec. fp-index added get_loc_tuple + get_loc tuple-aware overload + get_loc_level (partial tuple lookup) + slice_locs (sorted-MI range slicing). fp-frame added DataFrame::loc_tuple + loc_tuple_with_columns + xs + xs_level + get_loc + get_loc_level. Covers surfaces 1 (df.loc[(a,1)] — loc_tuple), 3 (df.xs(key, level=N) — xs_level), 4 (df.get_loc — get_loc), 5 (df.index.get_loc_level — get_loc_level), 6 (MultiIndex.slice_locs — slice_locs) from the bead spec; surface 2 (level slicing idx[('a', :)]) handled via loc_tuple_with_columns' partial-key semantics. 640 net lines added across fp-frame (+396) and fp-index (+244). Tests under rch: cargo test -p fp-frame --lib → 1411/0 (up 6 from 1405 pre-slice-3, confirming slice 3 test suite is in).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:57.596576518Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:00.884964566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:00.882480736Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","indexing","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.3","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:57.596576518Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.4","title":"[EPIC slice 5/6] IO formats write + read n-level row MultiIndex (CSV/Excel/Parquet/Feather/JSON)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Each IO format needs to read/write an n-level row MultiIndex frame and round-trip it:

1. CSV: write_csv_with_options(df, index=true) emits n leading columns (one per level); read_csv_with_options(index_col=[c1, c2, c3]) promotes them back. Matches pandas to_csv / read_csv with index_col as a list.
2. Excel: write_excel_bytes_with_options emits n leading columns; read_excel_bytes_with_options(index_col=[c1, c2, c3]) promotes them. Builds on the index-detection helper landed in br-ho6t (commit d0c5b2f) — extend detection to recognize multi-column writer-emitted RangeIndex tuples, not just single-column flat ones.
3. Parquet: ArrowSchema-level metadata for MultiIndex is pandas-standard (uses __index_level_N__ column naming). Emit and honor those metadata tags.
4. Feather / Arrow IPC: same __index_level_N__ metadata pattern as parquet.
5. JSON orients: records / index / split orient handling for n-level tuple keys (tuple -> array in JSON).

Acceptance: each format round-trips a 3-level row MultiIndex frame through write-then-read and asserts DataFrame::equals. Each format's existing proptest (prop_csv / prop_excel / prop_parquet / prop_feather / prop_json) extended to exercise the MultiIndex path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:35:45.578514806Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:32:39.262434033Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:32:39.260148055Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 22c7c3f","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","io","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:35:45.578514806Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","title":"[EPIC slice 6/6] Live-oracle conformance coverage for row MultiIndex (construction/groupby/indexing/reshape/io)","description":"Depends on slices 1-5.

Live-oracle conformance coverage for every row MultiIndex surface that slices 1-5 introduce, following the assert_live_oracle_* pattern used by br-fmmq / br-28y8 / br-339q / br-icm3 / br-i2j8:

1. Construction: DataFrame round-trips a 2-level and a 3-level row MultiIndex through its public constructors; matches pandas pd.DataFrame(..., index=pd.MultiIndex.from_tuples(...)) shape/labels.

2. GroupBy multi-key: groupby([k1, k2]).{sum, mean, min, max, count, std, var, median, first, last, agg_list, agg_named}() output matches pd.DataFrame.groupby([k1, k2]).*() under DataFrame::equals AND both carry a real row MultiIndex.

3. Indexing: df.loc[(a, 1)], df.loc[(a, slice(None))], df.xs(key, level=N), df.get_loc((a, 1)) — each matches pandas output.

4. Reshape: df.set_index([c1, c2]), df.reset_index(), df.stack(level), df.unstack(level), df.swaplevel(i, j), df.reorder_levels(order), df.droplevel(level) — each round-trips and matches pandas.

5. IO: CSV / Excel / Parquet / Feather / JSON write-then-read round-trips a row MultiIndex frame; each reader correctly reconstructs the MultiIndex from its format-specific metadata.

Each surface gets its own packet under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/, its own assert_live_oracle_* test in the tests block of crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs, and its own entry in the differential_all_packets_green aggregate. Target: 30-50 new fixtures total across the 5 groups; current bootstrap_cases sits at 1249 fixtures all green, this slice would bring it to ~1300.

Acceptance: all new live-oracle tests pass under rch AND packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases + differential_all_packets_green remain green.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","epic-child","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","title":"[EPIC slice 6/6] Live-oracle conformance coverage for row MultiIndex (construction/groupby/indexing/reshape/io)","description":"Depends on slices 1-5.

Live-oracle conformance coverage for every row MultiIndex surface that slices 1-5 introduce, following the assert_live_oracle_* pattern used by br-fmmq / br-28y8 / br-339q / br-icm3 / br-i2j8:

1. Construction: DataFrame round-trips a 2-level and a 3-level row MultiIndex through its public constructors; matches pandas pd.DataFrame(..., index=pd.MultiIndex.from_tuples(...)) shape/labels.

2. GroupBy multi-key: groupby([k1, k2]).{sum, mean, min, max, count, std, var, median, first, last, agg_list, agg_named}() output matches pd.DataFrame.groupby([k1, k2]).*() under DataFrame::equals AND both carry a real row MultiIndex.

3. Indexing: df.loc[(a, 1)], df.loc[(a, slice(None))], df.xs(key, level=N), df.get_loc((a, 1)) — each matches pandas output.

4. Reshape: df.set_index([c1, c2]), df.reset_index(), df.stack(level), df.unstack(level), df.swaplevel(i, j), df.reorder_levels(order), df.droplevel(level) — each round-trips and matches pandas.

5. IO: CSV / Excel / Parquet / Feather / JSON write-then-read round-trips a row MultiIndex frame; each reader correctly reconstructs the MultiIndex from its format-specific metadata.

Each surface gets its own packet under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/, its own assert_live_oracle_* test in the tests block of crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs, and its own entry in the differential_all_packets_green aggregate. Target: 30-50 new fixtures total across the 5 groups; current bootstrap_cases sits at 1249 fixtures all green, this slice would bring it to ~1300.

Acceptance: all new live-oracle tests pass under rch AND packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases + differential_all_packets_green remain green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:03:50.602978383Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:03:50.601114124Z","close_reason":"Shipped in live-oracle row MultiIndex coverage commit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","epic-child","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.6","title":"[EPIC slice 4/6] Reshape round-trips on row MultiIndex (set_index/reset_index/stack/unstack/swaplevel/reorder_levels)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Row MultiIndex reshape APIs that must survive round-trips:

1. df.set_index([c1, c2, c3]) — promotes n columns into an n-level row MultiIndex (today flattens to a single Utf8 column with a separator; see README.md:396 and br-frankenpandas-0yz7).
2. df.reset_index(level=None, drop=false) — inverse of set_index; drops all or selected row MultiIndex levels back to data columns. Must round-trip exactly with set_index.
3. df.stack(level) / df.unstack(level) — lifts a column into the row MultiIndex / drops a row MultiIndex level onto the column axis. Must round-trip exactly when level is unique.
4. df.swaplevel(i, j) on row axis — swaps two levels of the row MultiIndex (today only implemented on standalone MultiIndex, not on DataFrame.swaplevel).
5. df.reorder_levels(order) on row axis — permutes row MultiIndex levels by name or position.
6. df.droplevel(level) on row axis — drops a level, collapsing to a flat Index if only one level remains.

Acceptance: each of the 6 reshape operations round-trips a 3-level row MultiIndex frame through its inverse (or a pandas-parity reshape) under assert_live_oracle_* against the pandas oracle. Existing 387 fp-conformance lib tests green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:11.638150563Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:56:38.887324743Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:56:38.885490983Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9c3e5ec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","multi-index","reshape","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.6","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:11.638150563Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-21gk","title":"pandas: fp-columnar Column.searchsorted arraylike values parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:04:01.182809132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.254632112Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.252789152Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column.searchsorted arraylike values parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -87,8 +87,12 @@ def validate_pandas_module(pd_mod: Any) -> None:
 def label_from_json(value: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
     kind = value.get("kind")
     raw = value.get("value")
+    if kind == "bool":
+        return bool(raw)
     if kind == "int64":
         return int(raw)
+    if kind == "float64":
+        return float(raw)
     if kind == "utf8":
         return str(raw)
     raise OracleError(f"unsupported index label kind: {kind!r}")
@@ -134,12 +138,45 @@ def scalar_to_json(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
 
 def label_to_json(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
     if isinstance(value, bool):
-        return {"kind": "utf8", "value": str(value)}
+        return {"kind": "bool", "value": value}
     if isinstance(value, int):
         return {"kind": "int64", "value": value}
     return {"kind": "utf8", "value": str(value)}
 
 
+def tuple_label_to_flat_string(values: tuple[Any, ...]) -> str:
+    return "|".join(str(value) for value in values)
+
+
+def multiindex_from_json(pd, raw: dict[str, Any]):
+    tuples_raw = raw.get("tuples")
+    if not isinstance(tuples_raw, list):
+        raise OracleError("row_multiindex.tuples must be a list")
+
+    tuples: list[tuple[Any, ...]] = []
+    for position, tuple_raw in enumerate(tuples_raw):
+        if not isinstance(tuple_raw, list):
+            raise OracleError(
+                f"row_multiindex.tuples[{position}] must be a list of labels"
+            )
+        tuples.append(tuple(label_from_json(item) for item in tuple_raw))
+
+    names_raw = raw.get("names", [])
+    if not isinstance(names_raw, list):
+        raise OracleError("row_multiindex.names must be a list when provided")
+    names = [None if name is None else str(name) for name in names_raw]
+    return pd.MultiIndex.from_tuples(tuples, names=names or None)
+
+
+def multiindex_to_json(index) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return {
+        "tuples": [
+            [label_to_json(value) for value in values] for values in index.tolist()
+        ],
+        "names": [None if name is None else str(name) for name in index.names],
+    }
+
+
 def scalar_is_missing(value: Any) -> bool:
     return value is None or (isinstance(value, float) and math.isnan(value))
 
@@ -1785,6 +1822,7 @@ def dataframe_from_json(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]):
     columns_raw = payload.get("columns")
     column_order_raw = payload.get("column_order")
     categorical_columns_raw = payload.get("categorical_columns")
+    row_multiindex_raw = payload.get("row_multiindex")
     if not isinstance(index_raw, list):
         raise OracleError("frame payload requires index list")
     if not isinstance(columns_raw, dict):
@@ -1829,6 +1867,11 @@ def dataframe_from_json(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]):
     frame = pd.DataFrame(columns, index=index)
     frame = frame.reindex(columns=column_order)
 
+    if row_multiindex_raw is not None:
+        if not isinstance(row_multiindex_raw, dict):
+            raise OracleError("frame payload row_multiindex must be an object")
+        frame.index = multiindex_from_json(pd, row_multiindex_raw)
+
     if categorical_columns_raw is not None:
         if not isinstance(categorical_columns_raw, dict):
             raise OracleError("frame payload categorical_columns must be an object")
@@ -1873,11 +1916,18 @@ def dataframe_to_json(frame) -> dict[str, Any]:
         columns[key] = values
         column_order.append(key)
 
-    return {
+    response = {
         "index": [label_to_json(v) for v in frame.index.tolist()],
         "columns": columns,
         "column_order": column_order,
     }
+    if hasattr(frame.index, "nlevels") and getattr(frame.index, "nlevels", 1) > 1:
+        response["index"] = [
+            label_to_json(tuple_label_to_flat_string(values))
+            for values in frame.index.tolist()
+        ]
+        response["row_multiindex"] = multiindex_to_json(frame.index)
+    return response
 
 
 def require_expr_payload(payload: dict[str, Any], op_name: str) -> str:
@@ -2410,6 +2460,14 @@ def op_dataframe_memory_usage(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
 
 
+def op_dataframe_identity(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
+    if frame_payload is None:
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_identity requires frame payload")
+    frame = dataframe_from_json(pd, frame_payload)
+    return {"expected_frame": dataframe_to_json(frame)}
+
+
 def op_dataframe_round(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
     if frame_payload is None:
@@ -2670,25 +2728,37 @@ def op_dataframe_loc(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     labels = [label_from_json(item) for item in loc_labels]
 
     try:
-        out = frame.loc[labels]
+        if hasattr(frame.index, "nlevels") and getattr(frame.index, "nlevels", 1) > 1:
+            out = frame.loc[tuple(labels)]
+        else:
+            out = frame.loc[labels]
     except KeyError as exc:
         raise OracleError(f"dataframe_loc label lookup failed: {exc}") from exc
 
+    if not hasattr(out, "columns"):
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_loc currently requires DataFrame-shaped selections")
+
     return {"expected_frame": dataframe_to_json(out)}
 
 
 def op_dataframe_xs(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
     xs_key = payload.get("xs_key")
+    xs_level = payload.get("xs_level")
     if frame_payload is None:
         raise OracleError("dataframe_xs requires frame payload")
     if xs_key is None:
         raise OracleError("dataframe_xs requires xs_key payload")
+    if xs_level is not None and (isinstance(xs_level, bool) or not isinstance(xs_level, int)):
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_xs xs_level must be an integer when provided")
 
     frame = dataframe_from_json(pd, frame_payload)
     key = label_from_json(xs_key)
     try:
-        out = frame.xs(key)
+        if xs_level is None:
+            out = frame.xs(key)
+        else:
+            out = frame.xs(key, level=int(xs_level))
     except Exception as exc:
         raise OracleError(f"dataframe_xs failed: {exc}") from exc
 
@@ -2894,6 +2964,53 @@ def op_dataframe_groupby_sum(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     return {"expected_frame": dataframe_to_json(out)}
 
 
+def op_dataframe_groupby_agg_multi(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
+    groupby_columns = payload.get("groupby_columns")
+    agg_multi = payload.get("groupby_agg_multi")
+    observed = payload.get("groupby_observed", True)
+    if frame_payload is None:
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_groupby_agg_multi requires frame payload")
+    if not isinstance(groupby_columns, list) or not groupby_columns:
+        raise OracleError(
+            "dataframe_groupby_agg_multi requires non-empty groupby_columns list"
+        )
+    if not isinstance(agg_multi, dict) or not agg_multi:
+        raise OracleError(
+            "dataframe_groupby_agg_multi requires non-empty groupby_agg_multi object"
+        )
+    if not isinstance(observed, bool):
+        raise OracleError("dataframe_groupby_agg_multi groupby_observed must be a boolean")
+
+    columns = [str(entry).strip() for entry in groupby_columns]
+    if any(not entry for entry in columns):
+        raise OracleError(
+            "dataframe_groupby_agg_multi groupby_columns entries must be non-empty strings"
+        )
+
+    func_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
+    for raw_name, raw_funcs in agg_multi.items():
+        name = str(raw_name)
+        if not isinstance(raw_funcs, list) or not raw_funcs:
+            raise OracleError(
+                f"dataframe_groupby_agg_multi groupby_agg_multi[{name!r}] must be a non-empty list"
+            )
+        funcs = [str(func).strip() for func in raw_funcs]
+        if any(not func for func in funcs):
+            raise OracleError(
